factors.summary: Near-term risks include government budget constraints and customer funding delays.

evidence.text: Bookings are impacted by the timing and amounts of awards in a given period, which are subject to numerous factors, including the desired capability by the customer and urgency of customer needs; fiscal constraints placed on customer budgets; political uncertainty; the timing of customer negotiations; the timing of governmental approvals and notifications; and the timing of option exercises or increases in scope.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Government budget constraints and customer funding delays pose moderate headwinds.

evidence.text: The decrease in total net sales of $299 million in 2014 compared to 2013 was primarily due to lower intersegment sales of $163 million principally driven by lower volume on contracts supporting U.S. Army ground sensor systems, lower net sales of $144 million on classified programs, lower net sales of $101 million on certain intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance systems programs principally driven by reduced program requirements, and lower net sales of $68 million on integrated communications systems programs principally driven by lower U.S. Army and U.S. Air Force customer requirements on tactical communications systems.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining volume trends indicate a moderate headwind.

evidence.text: The net change in EAC adjustments was principally driven by labor and material production efficiencies throughout 2014 on two international tactical radar systems programs which amounted to $58 million, with the remainder of the change driven by efficiencies on certain classified programs in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Production efficiencies indicate moderate improvement in cost margins.

evidence.text: The net change in EAC adjustments was principally driven by labor and material production efficiencies throughout 2014 on two international tactical radar systems programs which amounted to $58 million, with the remainder of the change driven by efficiencies on certain classified programs in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity suggests a positive development in cost margins.

evidence.text: The decrease in backlog of $621 million or 9% at December 31, 2015 compared to December 31, 2014 was primarily due to sales in excess of bookings, principally within our Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance Systems and Tactical Airborne Systems product lines.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Shrinking backlog indicates a moderate headwind in demand.

factors.summary: Classified programs are contributing to revenue but not growing.

evidence.text: Higher net sales of $279 million on classified programs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Programs contribute to revenue but show no clear growth trend.

evidence.text: Forcepoint, formerly known as Raytheon|Websense, is a global provider of information technology security products and related services designed to protect commercial and government organizations and their customers and other users from external and internal threats, including modern cyber-threats, advanced malware attacks, information leaks, legal liability and productivity loss. Forcepoint is a joint venture company (with Vista Equity Partners) created in May 2015 through a series of transactions by which Raytheon acquired Websense from Vista Equity Partners and combined it with RCP, formerly part of the IIS segment, and then sold a minority interest in the combined company to Vista Equity Partners.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: M&A activity suggests strategic expansion and opportunity.

factors.summary: The backlog is showing mixed signals, with one subsection indicating growth due to increased bookings and delayed revenue recognition, while another subsection reports a decrease in backlog, primarily due to sales exceeding bookings in specific product lines.

evidence.text: Total backlog includes both funded backlog (firm orders for which funding is authorized, appropriated and contractually obligated by the customer but for which work has not been performed) and unfunded backlog (firm orders for which funding has not been appropriated and/or contractually obligated by the customer and for which work has not been performed).

The decrease in backlog of $621 million or 9% at December 31, 2015 compared to December 31, 2014 was primarily due to sales in excess of bookings, principally within our Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance Systems and Tactical Airborne Systems product lines.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from backlog growth and decrease indicate no clear trend.

factors.summary: Demand trends appear mixed, with stable bookings in certain areas but a deceleration in others. While there are significant bookings in key markets, there is evidence of lower net sales due to reduced program requirements and lower intersegment sales.

evidence.text: Bookings were relatively consistent with 2014. In 2015, IDS booked $2.0 billion to provide advanced Patriot air and missile defense capability for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and $769 million to provide advanced Patriot air and missile defense capability for the Republic of Korea.

Total net sales decrease d $276 million in 2015 compared to 2014. Included in the change in total net sales was lower net sales of $111 million primarily due to reduced schedule requirements on international tactical radar systems programs, lower intersegment sales of $65 million driven by lower volume on contracts supporting radar programs, and higher net sales of $279 million on classified programs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends show mixed signals with stable bookings in some areas but deceleration in others, indicating moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Input costs are showing mixed trends, with cost increases contributing to margin pressure in certain areas, while other areas are experiencing cost declines.

evidence.text: The increase in total operating expenses of $347 million in 2015 compared to 2014 was primarily due to an increase in materials and subcontractors costs of $328 million, driven principally by the activity on the international Patriot program awarded in the fourth quarter of 2014 and the international air and missile defense system program awarded in the fourth quarter of 2013 described above in Total Net Sales.

The decrease in materials and subcontractors costs was driven by the activity on the programs described above in Total Net Sales, with the remaining change spread across numerous programs with no individual or common significant driver.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs show mixed trends with increases in some areas and decreases in others, leading to no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure, though they are noted to be manageable. There is a tension between the increase in labor costs and their manageability.

evidence.text: The increase in total operating expenses of $347 million in 2015 compared to 2014 was primarily due to an increase in materials and subcontractors costs of $328 million, driven principally by the activity on the international Patriot program awarded in the fourth quarter of 2014 and the international air and missile defense system program awarded in the fourth quarter of 2013 described above in Total Net Sales.
The decrease in labor costs was principally driven by the activity on the programs described above in Total Net Sales.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure, though they are noted to be manageable, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Margins are decreasing, primarily driven by changes in EAC adjustments and volume changes. While there are mentions of mix and other performance factors, these do not fully offset the negative impact on margins.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating margin in 2015 compared to 2014 was primarily due to the change in mix and other performance.
The decrease in operating margin in 2015 compared to 2014 was primarily due to the net change in EAC adjustments, partially offset by the change in mix and other performance.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are decreasing due to changes in EAC adjustments and volume changes, with negative impacts outweighing positive factors.

factors.summary: Government budgets and customer funding appropriations are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Bookings are impacted by the timing and amounts of awards in a given period, which are subject to numerous factors, including... fiscal constraints placed on customer budgets; political uncertainty;... and the timing of governmental approvals and notifications.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Government budgets and customer funding appropriations are key sensitivities, posing significant risks to near-term results.
